prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI density-fitted) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: user-supplied fixed geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz; no re-optimization (single-point DFT on this geometry, i.e. DFT//XYZ)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 2 (open-shell doublet; unrestricted KS)
Software: PySCF backend via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill (see CLI below)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 12 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine. integrity.trustworthy = true.
Live log & files: calculation wrote a live progress log to the frontier-orbitals .out file announced when the run launched (tail-able during execution). Result JSON saved as tempo_orbitals.json; CLI invocation recorded inside that file for full reproducibility.

Headline result
HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans estimate) = 3.8724203 eV
• HOMO energy  = −6.3375439 eV (index 86)
• LUMO energy  = −2.4651235 eV (index 87)

This value was obtained with the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill using the command recorded in the job’s `cli_invocation` field:
/…/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 2 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz --out tempo_orbitals.json

All quantities are electronic energies referenced to the B3LYP/def2-TZVP energy zero; energies from different methods are not directly comparable without consistent references.
